steps.title: Identify the dominant structural pattern
steps.content: The poem describes Night with human attributes throughout: she wears a garment, draws a veil over her face, has her hair, and moves her hands. Every image maps an element of night onto part of a person's body or clothing. This consistent mapping is the poem's organizing structure.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It presents alternating descriptions of night in a rural area and in a city.’: the poem names no location at all — rural or urban. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It sketches an image of nightfall, then an image of sunrise.’: the sun or sunrise is never mentioned; only night is described. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It makes an extended comparison of night to a human being.’: garment, face, veil, hair, and hands all map night onto a person from start to finish. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘It portrays how night changes from one season of the year to the next.’: seasons are never referenced; the poem presents a single, static image of night. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
